name: Gary Smulyan
abstract: Gary Smulyan (born April 4, 1956) is an American jazz musician who plays baritone saxophone. He studied at Hofstra University before working with Woody Herman.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: I just did one three months ago with three baritones — Ronnie Cuber, Gary Smulyan and myself, and we did the music of Gerry Mulligan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: Well in the saxophones there’s Frank Wess and Dick Oatts, Ralph LaLama, Gary Smulyan. Slide Hampton’s in the band and he writes for the band too which is —
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Bergonzi
about_quotes.evidence: I heard the story that when Miles was playing at the Plugged Nickel the band got together and said, “Let’s play what we don’t hear.” And that doesn’t mean, like you were saying Gary Smulyan said you know play what you hear.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Bergonzi interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 7, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: I like Pepper a lot and I like Nick Brignola and Ronnie Cuber and Gary Smulyan and people like that playing today.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Galper
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] All right. Gary Smulyan, who told me jazz is four things...
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022
